timeline.summary: Diagnosis: Mild Hepatic Dysfunction. Found 3 similar cases with successful outcomes. Treatment: Monitor liver function closely; consider further imaging or referral to gastroenterology for persistent symptoms.

timeline.summary: Learned: Intermittent abdominal discomfort with nausea after meals requires ultrasound for cholecystitis workup. This will improve future cholecystitis diagnoses.

details.principle_text: Intermittent abdominal discomfort with slight nausea after meals requires ultrasound to assess for cholecystitis before concluding hepatic dysfunction.
details.reasoning: Initial assumption of hepatic dysfunction neglected the common presentation of cholecystitis; investigations must align with symptom patterns.
